id.title: id
id.description: ID is the unique identifier of the authorization.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description: ID is the unique identifier of the project the user was granted the authorization for.

projectName.title: project_name
projectName.description: Name is the name of the project the user was granted the authorization for.

projectOrganizationId.title: project_organization_id
projectOrganizationId.description: OrganizationID is the ID of the organization the project belongs to.

projectGrantId.title: project_grant_id
projectGrantId.description: ID of the granted project, only provided if it is a granted project.

grantedOrganizationId.title: granted_organization_id
grantedOrganizationId.description: ID of the organization the project is granted to, only provided if it is a granted project.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: The unique identifier of the organization the authorization belongs to.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp when the authorization was created.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description:
ChangeDate is the timestamp when the authorization was last updated.
 In case the authorization was not updated, this field is equal to the creation date.

state.title: state
state.description: State is the current state of the authorization.

roles.title: roles
roles.description: Roles contains the roles the user was granted for the project.

authorizationIds.title: authorization_ids
authorizationIds.description: Search for authorizations by their IDs.

inUserIds.title: in_user_ids
inUserIds.description: Search for authorizations by the IDs of the users who were granted the authorizations.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description: Search for an organizations authorizations by its ID.

projectGrantId.title: project_grant_id
projectGrantId.description:
Search for authorizations by the ID of the project grant the user was granted the authorization for.
 This will also include authorizations granted for project grants of the same project.

projectId.title: project_id
projectId.description:
Search for authorizations by the ID of the project the user was granted the authorization for.
 This will also include authorizations granted for project grants of the same project.

projectName.title: project_name
projectName.description:
Search for authorizations by the name of the project the user was granted the authorization for.
 This will also include authorizations granted for project grants of the same project.

roleKey.title: role_key
roleKey.description: Search for authorizations by the key of the role the user was granted.

state.title: state
state.description: Search for authorizations by their state.

userDisplayName.title: user_display_name
userDisplayName.description: Search for authorizations by the public display name of the granted user.

userId.title: user_id
userId.description: Search for authorizations by the ID of the user who was granted the authorization.

userOrganizationId.title: user_organization_id
userOrganizationId.description: Search for authorizations by the ID of the organisation the user is part of.

userPreferredLoginName.title: user_preferred_login_name
userPreferredLoginName.description: Search for authorizations by the preferred login name of the granted user.

name.title: name
name.description:
Specify the name of the organization the authorization was granted for to search for.
 This can either be the organization the project or the project grant is part of.

method.title: method
method.description:
Specify the method to search for the organization name. Default is EQUAL.
 For example, to search for all authorizations with an organization name containing a specific string,
 use CONTAINS or CONTAINS_IGNORE_CASE.

name.title: name
name.description:
Specify the name of the project the user was granted the authorization for to search for.
 Note that this will also include authorizations granted for project grants of the same project.

method.title: method
method.description:
Specify the method to search for the project name. Default is EQUAL.
 For example, to search for all authorizations granted on a project with
 a name containing a specific string, use CONTAINS or CONTAINS_IGNORE_CASE.

key.title: key
key.description: Specify the key of the role the user was granted to search for.

method.title: method
method.description:
Specify the method to search for the role key. Default is EQUAL.
 For example, to search for all authorizations starting with a specific role key,
 use STARTS_WITH or STARTS_WITH_IGNORE_CASE.

state.title: state
state.description: Specify the state of the authorization to search for.

id.title: id
id.description: ID represents the ID of the user who was granted the authorization.

preferredLoginName.title: preferred_login_name
preferredLoginName.description: PreferredLoginName represents the preferred login name of the granted user.

displayName.title: display_name
displayName.description: DisplayName represents the public display name of the granted user.

avatarUrl.title: avatar_url
avatarUrl.description: AvatarURL is the URL to the user's public avatar image.

organizationId.title: organization_id
organizationId.description:
The organization the user belong to.
 This does not have to correspond with the authorizations organization.

displayName.title: display_name
displayName.description: Specify the public display name of the granted user to search for.

method.title: method
method.description:
Specify the method to search for the display name. Default is EQUAL.
 For example, to search for all authorizations granted to a user with
 a display name containing a specific string, use CONTAINS or CONTAINS_IGNORE_CASE.

loginName.title: login_name
loginName.description: Specify the preferred login name of the granted user to search for.

method.title: method
method.description:
Specify the method to search for the preferred login name. Default is EQUAL.
 For example, to search for all authorizations granted to a user with
 a preferred login name containing a specific string, use CONTAINS or CONTAINS_IGNORE_CASE.

id.title: id
id.description: Only return resources that belong to this id.

ids.title: ids
ids.description: Defines the ids to query for.
